# human-context

> A single HTML file that turns any folder of Markdown docs into a navigable knowledge base. No server, no npm install, no build step. Files stay in your repo.

## What it does

Drop `md-folder-viewer.html` into any project. Open it in Chrome or Edge, point it at your folder, and get: file tree with token counts, syntax highlighting, TOC with scroll spy, cross-file navigation, dark mode, and multilingual UI (EN / Tiếng Việt / 中文). Nothing is uploaded — everything runs client-side.

## Source files

- [Full viewer source](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zzgiabaozzbui/human-context/main/md-folder-viewer.html): `md-folder-viewer.html` — the main tool, one self-contained HTML file, all logic inside
- [Simple reader](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zzgiabaozzbui/human-context/main/md-reader.html): `md-reader.html` — drag-and-drop single-file version, no folder needed
- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zzgiabaozzbui/human-context/main/README.md): full documentation, quick start, feature list, roadmap

## How it works

```
md-folder-viewer.html
├── File System Access API  → showDirectoryPicker() [Chrome/Edge]
├── webkitdirectory input   → fallback for Firefox
├── marked.js (CDN)         → markdown parsing
├── highlight.js (CDN)      → syntax highlighting
├── IndexedDB               → folder handle persistence across sessions
└── CSS custom properties   → full light/dark design system, no framework
```

## Quick start

1. Download `md-folder-viewer.html`
2. Open in Chrome or Edge (double-click)
3. Click **Open Folder** → select your project folder

Or via curl:

```bash
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zzgiabaozzbui/human-context/main/md-folder-viewer.html
```

## Optional: full content for AI

All readable content in one file: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zzgiabaozzbui/human-context/main/llms-full.txt
